([Urdu](/wiki/Urdu_language "Urdu language"): مقتدرہِ پانی و بجلی ترقی), colloquially known as **WAPDA**, is a Pakistani [government](/wiki/Government_of_Pakistan "Government of Pakistan")-owned [public utility](/wiki/Public_utility "Public utility") agency maintaining [hydropower](/wiki/Hydropower "Hydropower") and [water](/wiki/Water_industry "Water industry") in [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan"), although it does not manage [thermal power](/wiki/Thermal_power_station "Thermal power station") plants. WAPDA includes the [Tarbela](/wiki/Tarbela_Dam "Tarbela Dam") and [Mangla](/wiki/Mangla_Dam "Mangla Dam") dams among its most notable resources. It is headquartered in [Lahore](/wiki/Lahore "Lahore"). The current Chairman of WAPDA is Lt. General (R) [Muhammad Saeed](/wiki/Muhammad_Saeed_(general) "Muhammad Saeed (general)"), who has held the position since August 2025.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Water & Power Development Authority](/w/index.php?title=Water_%26_Power_Development_Authority&redirect=no "Water & Power Development Authority"))

Government agency of Pakistan

For the successor of WAPDA in East Pakistan (now Bangladesh), see [Bangladesh Power Development Board](/wiki/Bangladesh_Power_Development_Board "Bangladesh Power Development Board").

Water & Power Development Authority

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| مقتدرہِ پانی و بجلی ترقی | |
| Emblem of WAPDA | |
| [WAPDA House](/wiki/WAPDA_House "WAPDA House"), [Lahore](/wiki/Lahore "Lahore")-54000, Pakistan | |
| Agency overview | |
| Formed | February 22, 1958; 68 years ago (1958-02-22) |
| Type | [Power](/wiki/Electric_power "Electric power") and [Hydrology](/wiki/Hydrology "Hydrology") |
| Jurisdiction | [Federal Government of Pakistan](/wiki/Government_of_Pakistan "Government of Pakistan") |
| Headquarters | [WAPDA House](/wiki/WAPDA_House "WAPDA House"), [Lahore](/wiki/Lahore "Lahore")-54000, [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan") |
| Agency executive | * [Lt Gen](/wiki/Lt_Gen "Lt Gen") [Muhammad Saeed](/wiki/Muhammad_Saeed "Muhammad Saeed")[[1]](#cite_note-1), [Chairman](/wiki/Chairman "Chairman") of Wapda with BPS 22 |
| Parent agency | [Ministry of Water Resources](/wiki/Ministry_of_Water_Resources_(Pakistan) "Ministry of Water Resources (Pakistan)") |
| Website | [www.wapda.gov.pk](http://www.wapda.gov.pk/) |

The **Pakistan Water & Power Development Authority** ([Urdu](/wiki/Urdu_language "Urdu language"): مقتدرہِ پانی و بجلی ترقی), colloquially known as **WAPDA**, is a Pakistani [government](/wiki/Government_of_Pakistan "Government of Pakistan")-owned [public utility](/wiki/Public_utility "Public utility") agency maintaining [hydropower](/wiki/Hydropower "Hydropower") and [water](/wiki/Water_industry "Water industry") in [Pakistan](/wiki/Pakistan "Pakistan"), although it does not manage [thermal power](/wiki/Thermal_power_station "Thermal power station") plants. WAPDA includes the [Tarbela](/wiki/Tarbela_Dam "Tarbela Dam") and [Mangla](/wiki/Mangla_Dam "Mangla Dam") dams among its most notable resources. It is headquartered in [Lahore](/wiki/Lahore "Lahore"). The current Chairman of WAPDA is Lt. General (R) [Muhammad Saeed](/wiki/Muhammad_Saeed_(general) "Muhammad Saeed (general)"), who has held the position since August 2025.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Water_and_Power_Development_Authority&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

WAPDA was established by an act of parliament in 1958[[5]](#cite_note-5) to unify the maintenance of [infrastructure](/wiki/Infrastructure "Infrastructure") previously overseen by [provincial](/wiki/Subdivisions_of_Pakistan "Subdivisions of Pakistan") agencies. Its chairmen included notable civil servants such as [Ghulam Ishaq Khan](/wiki/Ghulam_Ishaq_Khan "Ghulam Ishaq Khan"), [Ghulam Faruque Khan](/wiki/Ghulam_Faruque_Khan "Ghulam Faruque Khan"), and [Aftab Ghulam Nabi Kazi](/wiki/Aftab_Ghulam_Nabi_Kazi "Aftab Ghulam Nabi Kazi"), who later served as [President of Pakistan](/wiki/President_of_Pakistan "President of Pakistan"), Minister for Commerce, and Economic Adviser, respectively. In October 2007, the management of thermal power was transferred to the newly formed [Pakistan Electric Power Company](/wiki/Pakistan_Electric_Power_Company "Pakistan Electric Power Company") (PEPCO).

## WAPDA Water vision 2025

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Water_and_Power_Development_Authority&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: WAPDA Water vision 2025")]

WAPDA formulated a comprehensive $25–33 billion National Water Resource and Hydropower Development Programme, entitled Water Vision 2025. The Water Vision 2025 projects are expected to generate 16,000 MW of [hydroelectricity](/wiki/Hydroelectricity "Hydroelectricity"). Other goals are to prevent water shortages, limit drought and increase water storage for a growing population. Five massive hydropower projects have been announced by the President of Pakistan; these are to be completed by 2016, with a generation capacity of 9,500 MW. Two of the projects are ready for construction, while three are in the stages of feasibility studies and preparation of tender documents.[[6]](#cite_note-TheNation-6)[[7]](#cite_note-TheNation2-7)

## Sporting teams

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Water_and_Power_Development_Authority&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Sporting teams")]

Further information: [Water and Power Development Authority cricket team](/wiki/Water_and_Power_Development_Authority_cricket_team "Water and Power Development Authority cricket team") and [WAPDA F.C.](/wiki/WAPDA_F.C. "WAPDA F.C.")

## Hospitals

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Water_and_Power_Development_Authority&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Hospitals")]

There is a 260 Bedded WAPDA Teaching Hospital Complex at Lahore providing medical services to those in need. Additionally, there are 50 bedded hospitals at Tarbela, Peshawar, Gujranwala, Faisalabad, Hyderabad, Multan, Quetta & Guddu and 20 bedded hospital at Mangla and Sukkur which are fully functional and proving in-house services in all major clinical disciplines.

## Key WAPDA Chairmen Since Establishment

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Water_and_Power_Development_Authority&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Key WAPDA Chairmen Since Establishment")]

| Period | Chairman | Highlights |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1958 | Ghulam Faruque Khan | Chairman of East Pakistan WAPDA |
| 1961 | Ghulam Ishaq Khan | Oversaw Mangla & Warsak Dam projects |
| 1960s (exact date unspecified) | Aftab Ghulam Nabi Kazi | Honoured for Mangla Dam inauguration |
| ~2003 | Zafar Mehmood | Resigned citing personal reasons |
| 2016 – 2022 | Lt.General (R) [Muzammil Hussain](/wiki/Muzammil_Hussain_(general) "Muzammil Hussain (general)") | Spearheaded “Decade of Dams” & green Eurobond |
| Aug 2022 – Jun 2025 | Lt.General (R) [Sajjad Ghani](/wiki/Sajjad_Ghani "Sajjad Ghani") (23rd Chairman) | Continued infrastructure expansion |
| June 2025- August 2025 | Naveed Asghar Chaudhry (Acting) |  |
| 4 August 2025 | Lt.General (R) [Muhammad Saeed](/wiki/Muhammad_Saeed_(general) "Muhammad Saeed (general)") |  |
